ModuleName: 4 - Database Basics and Relational Data Concepts

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: A structured outline of the module's learning topics spanning database fundamentals, relational concepts, tables, keys, and schemas, used by instructors to plan content and by learners to navigate what will be covered.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: A navigational overview of the 'Database Basics and Relational Data Concepts' module listing and summarising its core topics — including what databases are, how they differ from spreadsheets and flat files, and foundational relational data concepts — which learners and instructors can use to preview the module's scope and structure before engaging with detailed content.

Artifacts.Name: Database_Basics_and_generated.pptx
Artifacts.Description: A slide deck introducing relational database concepts—including what databases are, how they differ from spreadsheets, and how tables, rows, columns, and keys organize and connect data—used by instructors to deliver lectures or by learners for self-study.

Artifacts.Name: Introduction to Databases
Artifacts.Description: This reading introduces what databases are, why they exist, and how they store and organize data, serving as a foundational orientation for learners beginning the module.

Artifacts.Name: Relational Database Concepts
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains the relational database model — including how data is organized into tables connected through shared values — tracing the concept from its origins to its role in modern enterprise systems, for learners building core conceptual knowledge.

Artifacts.Name: Tables, Rows, and Columns
Artifacts.Description: This reading covers the structure of database tables, explaining how rows and columns function as the primary organizational units for storing records, and is used by learners to understand how data is physically arranged within a relational database.

Artifacts.Name: Keys and Identifiers
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains the role of keys and identifiers in relational databases — including primary keys, foreign keys, and composite keys — covering how they uniquely identify rows and link tables, for learners who need to understand data integrity and navigation.

Artifacts.Name: Database Schemas
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains what a database schema is, how it serves as the structural blueprint governing a database's organization and validation rules, and is used by learners to understand how databases are designed before data is ever entered.

Artifacts.Name: Relationships Between Tables
Artifacts.Description: This reading covers the types of relationships between database tables — such as one-to-one, one-to-many, and many-to-many — explaining why data is split across focused tables and how those relationships form the foundation of the relational model.

Artifacts.Name: Foundations for SQL Querying
Artifacts.Description: This reading introduces SQL as the standard language for querying relational databases, explaining the conceptual groundwork learners need — including how data is organized and accessed — before writing their first queries.

Artifacts.Name: Foundations for SQL Querying
Artifacts.Description: This reading introduces SQL as the standard language for querying relational databases, explaining the conceptual groundwork learners need — including how data is organized and accessed — before writing their first queries.

Artifacts.Name: Quiz Question Bank (20 questions)
Artifacts.Description: A 20-question bank assessing learners on core database concepts such as the definition of a database, differences between databases and spreadsheets, flat file limitations, the origins of the relational model, and the role of primary keys, used to build quizzes or tests for the module.
